channel.title: +Sustainability on Vishvas's notes
channel.description: Recent content in +Sustainability on Vishvas's notes

item.title: Global resource-collapse
item.description: Energy and minerals Energy and minerals drive modernity, science, convenience, space exploration etc.. Pre-collapse Human trajectory Our planet (+ barren mars, hypothetically) is finite. Can’t add in other star systems - too far away to benefit those here. We grew exponentially, exploiting and even recklessly wasting resources. So, cannot sustain current rate of resource exploitation and pollution. After that will be a grind down to stone age before ultimate extinction.

item.title: Land-use
item.description: Cattle Feeding cows, buffaloes etc for meat & dairy is very inefficient if goal is to maximize population while minimizing environmental destruction. See Akira Hayami on decline of domestic animals in Edo era. Currently, most of our agriculture land is for feeding our livestock, not feeding humans.

item.title: Misc-fuels
item.description: H2 Termites are being researched as a possible renewable energy source as they can produce up to 2 liters of hydrogen from ingesting a single sheet of paper, making them one of the planet’s most efficient bioreactors
